page.title: Tools
page.subtitle: Tools are the smallest unit of action an agent can take. Built-in tools ship with Sparo OS; MCP tools come from external services you connect.

detail.selectHint: Select a tool on the left to see its documentation.

empty.title: No MCP servers yet
empty.hint: Add an MCP server to extend available tools.

server.noTools: This server has not exposed any tools yet.
server.deleteConfirmMessage: "{{id}}" will be removed. This cannot be undone.

editor.title: MCP JSON Configuration
editor.hint: Use the standard mcpServers format. Saving will restart affected servers.

AgentDispatch.name: AgentDispatch
AgentDispatch.summary: Route a user request to the best-matching agent.
AgentDispatch.what: Inspects the current request and routes it to the most suitable agent (Code, Cowork, QA, etc.) in Dispatcher mode. Returns the chosen agent and rationale.
when: Unclear which agent should handle a new task
whenNot: You already know which agent to use
Single-turn quick answers that do not need routing

related.name: Task
related.note: Actually hand off a bounded sub-task to a subagent.

AgentDispatch.notes: Dispatcher-only; usually not enabled in ordinary worker agents.

LS.name: LS
LS.what: Lists files and subdirectories at a given path. Useful as the first step of project exploration.
when: Getting a quick overview of a folder
Before deciding which files to read
whenNot: Searching by name pattern — use Glob
Searching by content — use Grep

Read.name: Read
Read.summary: Read the content of a file.
Read.what: Reads a file from the workspace and returns its content with line numbers. Supports offset/limit for large files.
whenNot: Binary files are best previewed by the file viewer
Full-tree search — use Grep

related.name: Grep
related.note: Search first to locate the lines to read.

Glob.name: Glob
Glob.what: Matches paths using glob syntax such as **/*.tsx. Returns files sorted by modification time.
when: Narrowing the search space before Grep
whenNot: Searching file contents — use Grep

related.name: Grep
related.note: Run content search on the matched subset.

Grep.name: Grep
Grep.what: Runs a ripgrep search across the workspace. Supports regex, file filters, case options, and context lines.
whenNot: You only need filenames — use Glob
Single-file quick scan — use Read

related.name: Read
related.note: Open a specific match to see full context.

Write.name: Write
Write.summary: Create or fully overwrite a file.
Write.what: Writes the given content to a file, creating it if necessary. Previous content is replaced.
whenNot: Editing an existing file partially — use Edit
Modifying binaries

related.name: Edit
related.note: Safer for local edits to existing files.

Write.notes: Destructive by design — always confirm intent before use.

Edit.name: Edit
Edit.summary: Apply exact string replacement to a file.
Edit.what: Replaces an exact, unique string inside a file. Preserves surrounding content and original line endings.
when: Renaming or patching a specific region
whenNot: Rewriting large portions — prefer Write
Changes where anchors are not unique

related.name: Read
related.note: Read first to craft a unique anchor.

Delete.name: Delete
Delete.summary: Delete a file from the workspace.
Delete.what: Removes a file path. Fails gracefully if the path does not exist.
whenNot: Uncertain whether the file is still referenced
Delete.notes: Irreversible — prefer a commit before batch deletes.

Bash.name: Bash
Bash.summary: Run a shell command in a workspace terminal.
Bash.what: Executes a shell command and streams stdout/stderr. Supports background mode for long-running processes.
whenNot: File operations available as dedicated tools — prefer Read/Edit/LS
Bash.notes: Can affect the host system — treat as dangerous and confirm destructive commands.

TerminalControl.name: TerminalControl
TerminalControl.what: Reads output, writes input, and terminates background terminals started by Bash.
whenNot: One-shot commands — just use Bash

SessionControl.name: SessionControl
SessionControl.summary: Control flow of the current agent session.
SessionControl.what: Pauses, resumes, or ends the current session under well-defined conditions.
whenNot: Ordinary turns — the coordinator handles flow automatically

SessionMessage.name: SessionMessage
SessionMessage.summary: Post a structured message to the session transcript.
SessionMessage.what: Emits a first-class message (status, notice, summary) into the current session for the user to see.
whenNot: Normal assistant replies — just answer

SessionHistory.name: SessionHistory
SessionHistory.summary: Recall earlier turns in the session.
SessionHistory.what: Returns a condensed view of earlier messages in the current session.
whenNot: Looking outside the current session — use project search tools

TodoWrite.name: TodoWrite
TodoWrite.summary: Maintain a structured todo list for the current task.
TodoWrite.what: Creates or updates an ordered list of todos with states (pending / in_progress / completed / cancelled).
whenNot: Trivial single-step tasks

related.name: CreatePlan
related.note: For larger plans that deserve a document.

Cron.name: Cron
Cron.what: Creates, lists and deletes scheduled jobs that fire prompts into a session at a given time or interval.
whenNot: One-off immediate actions

Task.name: Task
Task.summary: Delegate a bounded sub-task to a subagent.
Task.what: Launches a specialized subagent with its own prompt and scope. Returns the subagent's final result.
whenNot: Work that needs tight back-and-forth with the user

related.name: AgentDispatch
related.note: Route before delegating when the target is ambiguous.

Skill.name: Skill
Skill.what: Runs a registered skill profile that bundles prompts, tool policies and knowledge.
when: Repeatable workflows that already have a skill
whenNot: One-off work that does not justify a skill

AskUserQuestion.name: AskUserQuestion
AskUserQuestion.summary: Ask the user a multiple-choice question.
AskUserQuestion.what: Prompts the user with a structured question and discrete options; blocks further tool calls until answered.
whenNot: You can safely make an assumption and proceed

WebSearch.name: WebSearch
WebSearch.what: Runs a web search and returns a list of results with titles and snippets.
whenNot: Project-internal questions — use Grep

WebFetch.name: WebFetch
WebFetch.summary: Fetch a URL as readable text.
WebFetch.what: Downloads a webpage and returns it as markdown. Useful after WebSearch picks the best link.
when: Reading an article or docs page
Extracting structured text from a URL
whenNot: Binary assets or files behind auth

related.name: WebSearch
related.note: Find the URL first, then fetch it.

ListMCPResources.name: ListMCPResources
ListMCPResources.summary: List resources exposed by an MCP server.
ListMCPResources.what: Discovers the resources (files, datasets, documents) an MCP server exposes for reading.
when: Exploring what an MCP server offers

ReadMCPResource.name: ReadMCPResource
ReadMCPResource.what: Fetches the content of a resource exposed by an MCP server.

ListMCPPrompts.name: ListMCPPrompts
ListMCPPrompts.summary: List prompt templates exposed by an MCP server.
ListMCPPrompts.what: Discovers the prompt templates an MCP server offers for use by the agent.

GetMCPPrompt.name: GetMCPPrompt
GetMCPPrompt.what: Renders a server-supplied prompt template with the given arguments.

GenerativeUI.name: GenerativeUI
GenerativeUI.summary: Render a live interactive widget in the chat.
GenerativeUI.what: Produces a small interactive UI (form, choice widget, progress card) directly inline in the conversation.
when: Asking for structured input beyond yes/no
Showing progress of a long operation

GetFileDiff.name: GetFileDiff
GetFileDiff.summary: Get the unified diff for a file.
GetFileDiff.what: Returns the unified diff between the workspace version and a given baseline for a file.

Log.name: Log
Log.what: Writes a structured log entry for later inspection. Useful for debugging complex agent runs.

CreatePlan.name: CreatePlan
CreatePlan.summary: Create a plan document for a task.
CreatePlan.what: Creates or updates a plan markdown document describing the goals, steps and risks of a task.

related.name: TodoWrite
related.note: Convert plan steps into runtime todos.

CodeReview.name: CodeReview
CodeReview.summary: Submit a code review against recent changes.
CodeReview.what: Runs a code review pass over the recent diff and returns findings.

InitLiveApp.name: InitLiveApp
InitLiveApp.summary: Initialize a new Live App scaffold.
InitLiveApp.what: Bootstraps a new Live App project with a default template.

ComputerUse.name: ComputerUse
ComputerUse.what: Unified desktop automation: click, mouse move, scroll, drag, screenshot, locate-on-screen, key chord, type text, wait.
when: Driving a GUI app that has no API
whenNot: Anything reachable via CLI or filesystem
ComputerUse.notes: Can affect any application on the host — treat as dangerous and confirm before destructive actions.
